Lithium: Pegmatite (hard rock spodumene) or Lithium-rich brine in salars
Cobalt: Sediment-hosted copper-cobalt deposits or nickel laterite by-product
Graphite (Natural): Flake graphite in metamorphic rocks (schist, gneiss)
Copper: Porphyry copper deposits, sediment-hosted, or VMS
Manganese: Sedimentary manganese deposits or lateritic weathering
Neodymium (Rare Earth): Carbonatite or alkaline igneous deposits (bastnäsite, monazite)
Zinc: Sediment-hosted massive sulfide (SHMS), VMS, or MVT deposits

Lithium: Open-pit mining of pegmatite or pumping of lithium-rich brine
Cobalt: Open-pit or underground mining of copper-cobalt ore
Graphite (Natural): Open-pit or underground mining of graphite ore
Neodymium (Rare Earth): Open-pit or underground mining of rare earth ore
Zinc: Underground or open-pit mining of zinc ore (sphalerite)

Lithium:
Hard rock: Crushing + flotation → Spodumene concentrate (5.5–6.5% Li₂O)
Brine: Solar evaporation ponds
Cobalt: Crushing, grinding, flotation or leaching → Mixed Hydroxide Precipitate (MHP)
Nickel:
Laterite: HPAL or ferronickel smelting
Sulfide: Froth flotation → Nickel concentrate
Graphite (Natural): Crushing, grinding, flotation → Flake graphite concentrate (80–95% C)
Copper: Crushing, grinding, froth flotation → Copper concentrate (20–30% Cu)
Manganese: Crushing, screening, gravity/magnetic separation → Manganese concentrate
Silicon (Metallurgical/Solar): Crushing and screening to quartz lumps
Neodymium (Rare Earth): Crushing, grinding, flotation/gravity separation → Rare earth concentrate
Zinc: Crushing, grinding, froth flotation → Zinc concentrate (50–60% Zn)

Lithium:
Hard rock: Sulfuric acid roasting + leaching
Brine: Direct Lithium Extraction (DLE)
Cobalt: Smelting or HPAL → Cobalt matte or mixed sulfide/hydroxide
Nickel:
Laterite: HPAL → Mixed Hydroxide Precipitate (MHP)
Sulfide: Flash smelting → Nickel matte
Copper: Smelting → Copper matte then blister copper (98–99%)
Manganese: Smelting in electric arc furnace → Ferromanganese or Silicomanganese
Silicon (Metallurgical/Solar): Carbothermic reduction in submerged arc furnace → Metallurgical Grade Silicon (MG-Si)
Neodymium (Rare Earth): Cracking / leaching → Mixed rare earth solution or carbonate
Zinc: Roasting → Zinc oxide, then leaching → Zinc sulfate solution

Lithium: Conversion to battery-grade Lithium Carbonate (Li₂CO₃) or Lithium Hydroxide (LiOH)
Cobalt: Solvent extraction + electrowinning → Cobalt sulfate (CoSO₄) or high-purity metal
Nickel: Refining of matte/MHP → Class 1 Nickel or Nickel Sulfate
Graphite (Natural): Coating / surface treatment for battery anodes
Manganese: Electrolytic process → Electrolytic Manganese Metal (EMM) or Manganese Sulfate
Silicon (Metallurgical/Solar): Siemens process or fluidized bed reactor → Solar Grade Silicon

Lithium: Battery-grade Lithium Carbonate or Lithium Hydroxide
Graphite (Natural): Battery-grade Spherical Graphite or Purified Flake Graphite
Neodymium (Rare Earth): Neodymium Metal or NdFeB magnet alloy
